case_title: CORAZON ZAMBRANO et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN et al

NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT OF ENTIRE CASE FILED BY PLAINTIFF ZAMBRANO, CORAZON ZAMBRANO, CESAR
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

QUIET TITLE - REAL PROPERTY, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ZAMBRANO, CORAZON ZAMBRANO, CESAR AS TO DEFENDANT HEYDAYIAN, NADER NOURI, HAM AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST DOES 1 TO 20, INCL. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR APR-13-2012 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JAN-13-2012 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         MAR-29-2012

APPLICATION FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ZAMBRANO, CORAZON ORDER FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS GRANTED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68634 (E), CRC 3.52

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NADER HEYDAYIAN, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST, AND HAM NOURI, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE MOTION SERVES NO USEFUL PURPOSE. MOREOVER, ABSENT SOME DISCERNABLE PREJUDICE TO THE OPPOSING PARTY, AND NONE WAS SHOWN HERE, SURPLUS ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO STRIKE. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED DEFENDANTS HAVE 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-18-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-29-13 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NADER HEYDAYIAN, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST, AND HAM NOURI, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Raquel Fox of the Tenderloin Housing Clinic (771.9850 x115) for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The motion serves no useful purpose. Moreover, absent some discernable prejudice to the opposing party, and none was shown here, surplus allegations are not subject to a motion to strike. Further, the court indicated defendants have 10 days leave to answer. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
